description: Pre-built assignment rule templates for Lead and Case objects

templates.name: Lead Assignment by Industry & Geography
templates.description: Route leads to regional/industry-specific teams based on industry and geographic location
templates.useCase: Multi-regional sales teams with industry specialization

entries.description: Technology industry leads in New York

configurationInstructions: Replace Queue IDs (00G_*) with your org's actual queue IDs
Adjust industries and states to match your business regions
Add more specific entries before general ones to maintain proper order
Consider adding email templates for high-value combinations
notes: Entry order is critical - specific combinations must come before general state-only entries
Catch-all entry (no criteria) prevents unassigned leads

templates.name: Case Assignment by Priority
templates.description: Route cases based on priority level, sending high-priority cases to senior support agents
templates.useCase: Support teams with tiered escalation based on case priority

entries.description: High-priority cases to senior support agent
Medium-priority cases to general support queue

configurationInstructions: Replace User ID (005_*) with actual senior support agent user ID
Replace Queue IDs (00G_*) with your org's support queue IDs
Create email template for high-priority notifications (00X_*)
Adjust priority values if using custom picklist values (default: High, Medium, Low)
notes: High-priority cases assigned to individual user for immediate attention
Email notification sent only for high-priority cases
Medium and low-priority cases routed to queues for team distribution

templates.name: Lead Assignment by Source & Rating
templates.description: Route high-quality leads from specific sources to senior sales representatives
templates.useCase: Sales teams prioritizing referrals and high-rated leads

entries.description: Hot referral leads to senior sales rep

configurationInstructions: Replace User ID (005_*) with senior sales representative user ID
Replace Queue IDs (00G_*) with your org's sales queue IDs
Create email template for hot referral notifications
Adjust LeadSource values to match your org's picklist options
Adjust Rating values if using custom picklist (default: Hot, Warm, Cold)
notes: Most valuable leads (Referral + Hot) get immediate senior rep attention
Entry order ensures hot referrals don't fall into general hot leads queue

templates.name: Case Assignment by Type & Origin
templates.description: Route cases based on case type and origin channel (Web, Phone, Email)
templates.useCase: Support teams with channel-specific and technical specialization
configurationInstructions: Replace Queue IDs (00G_*) with your org's support queue IDs
Adjust Type values to match your org's case types (default: Technical, Administrative, Other)
Adjust Origin values to match your org's options (default: Web, Phone, Email)
Add more specific combinations as needed (e.g., Technical + Phone)
notes: Prioritizes technical web cases for specialized handling
Phone cases get immediate attention regardless of type
Template is easily extensible for additional type/origin combinations

templates.name: Lead Assignment by Geography Only
templates.description: Simple geographic routing for distributed sales teams
templates.useCase: Sales teams organized purely by geographic territory
configurationInstructions: Replace Queue IDs (00G_*) with your org's regional queue IDs
Add entries for all key states/regions in your market
Consider grouping states into regions (West, East, Central, etc.)
Catch-all entry handles leads from states without specific queues
notes: Simplest template - single criterion per entry
Easy to extend by adding more state entries
No industry or other segmentation - purely geographic

templates.name: Case Assignment by Account Tier
templates.description: Route cases based on account tier/segment (Enterprise, Mid-Market, SMB)
templates.useCase: Support teams with dedicated enterprise support agents
configurationInstructions: Replace Queue IDs (00G_*) with your org's tiered support queue IDs
Adjust Account.Type values to match your org's account types
Create email template for enterprise case notifications
Consider adding priority-based entries within each tier
notes: Requires Case to have Account lookup populated
Enterprise cases can optionally include email notifications

usageInstructions.applyTemplate: Use assignment-rule-deployer.js or sfdc-assignment-rules-manager agent to apply templates
usageInstructions.customization: All templates require ID replacement (Users, Queues, Email Templates) and field value adjustment
usageInstructions.testing: Always test in sandbox before production deployment
usageInstructions.validation: Run assignee-validator.js to verify all referenced entities exist and are active

addGeographicCoverage.description: Add more states/regions to geographic templates
addGeographicCoverage.example: Add Entry for 'State = FL' with appropriate queue

addIndustrySegmentation.description: Add industry-specific entries to existing templates
addIndustrySegmentation.example: Add 'Industry = Financial Services' criteria to geographic entries

addPriorityEscalation.description: Add priority-based routing within existing templates
addPriorityEscalation.example: Add Entry 1: 'Priority = High AND State = CA' before general CA entry

addRecordTypeHandling.description: Add record type filtering to templates
addRecordTypeHandling.example: Add 'RecordTypeId = 012...' criteria to differentiate Partner vs Direct leads

commonPitfalls.orderingIssues: Ensure specific entries (more criteria) come before general entries (fewer criteria)
commonPitfalls.missingCatchAll: Always include a catch-all entry (no criteria) at the end to prevent unassigned records
commonPitfalls.inactiveAssignees: Validate all Users are Active and all Queues have the object enabled
commonPitfalls.accessIssues: Verify assignees have Edit access to the object (required for ownership)
commonPitfalls.emailTemplateErrors: Ensure email templates exist before deployment if referenced
